channel.title: SCP Foundation - new forum threads
channel.description: Threads in forums of the site "SCP Foundation" - Secure, Contain, Protect

item.title: A Baby Toy
item.description: A baby toy with animals that play with children.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a spherical baby toy that has multiple buttons on its surface that look like different animals. All of these buttons have been classified SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-5, and it also has a spiraling roulette with a turtle as the arrow which is classified as SCP-XXXX-6. When SCP-XXXX is near the vicinity of a child under the age of 4 years SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-6 will become animated this is its active phase, this will not happen if there is any individual above 4 years present in the room ,nor will it happen if there are any video recorders present in the room though audio recorders doesn't stop the active phase from happening.During SCP-XXXX active phase SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-5 will proceed to play with the children(s) ,or play with each other though ,SCP-XXXX-6 will not engage instead warning, or scolding the other SCP-XXXX instances if they make acts that could harm the children(s).

Description of SCP-XXXX-1 :has the appearance of a red cat with a joyful smile.
Description of SCP-XXXX-2 :has the appearance of a brown bear in a laid down position.
Description of SCP-XXXX-3 :has the appearance of a purple bird with its wings being fully shown.
Description of SCP-XXXX-4 :has the appearance of a blue dog that is running happily.
Description of SCP-XXXX-5 :has the appearance of a white rabbit in the middle of jumping.
Description of SCP-XXXX-6 :has the appearance of a turtle laid down on their underside.

[[collapsible]]

Central Narrative:
The central narrative of this piece would take the form of 1 audio recording with the SCP-XXXX instances,and 1 interview with them.
1 3 year old named David coming close to his birthday thus meeting SCP-XXXX requirement was put inside a room with SCP-XXXX ,and a audio recorder was hidden on the side of a beanie bag.(note:He was not very interested in SCP-XXXX)

David:What's happening to that toy?

SCP-XXXX-2:Well hello there buddy ,want to come play with us?

SCP-XXXX-3:Yeah you can join!!!…as long as you aren't rowdy as willy wagtail over there.

SCP-XXXX-4:Mind repeating that Miss Mary Quack?

SCP-XXXX-3:uhhh i didn't say anything.

David:Am i not supposed to be here?

SCP-XXXX-4:Sorry don't mind us, so are you going to play or no?

David:Yeah!so what game are we playing?

Hook/Attention Grabber: It's a baby toy ,and it has animals that is friendly to children

Additional Notes: i wanted to show how the audio log will look like sorry.

item.title: Pelicans we, an SCP Article Proposal
item.description: My first SCP article idea.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: yes

Page type: SCP article

Elevator pitch: This SCP is a Stuffed Pelican that causes any individual who touches it, or is too close to begin to hallucinate that every living being is a pelican, as well as hearing the song "Pelicans we" by Cosmo Sheldrake at high volumes to the point that it hurts the ears and causes headaches. Sometimes also causes blurry vision. This SCP is to be only handled by an individual wearing a sealed HAZMAT suit and is kept in a sealed glass container with a danger line on the floor marking the range of effect.

Central Narrative: This SCP is marked as Safe and/or Explained as it was discovered that it was manufactured in the 1960s and was held in possession of a prolific drug user who primarily used inhalable hallucinogens. The drugs and the anomalous energy caused by the drug use infused into the pelican causing it's effects. It has not been neutralized or destroyed, and research is going towards neutralization.

Hook/Attention Grabber: Not many "explained" SCPs are in effect currently (at least that I'm aware of, there are too many to be fully aware of all of them now) and it uses a historical period where drugs were common. There is still an anomalous nature that is common in SCP articles, so it works well with fitting in with the rest of them as well as being it's own thing.

Additional notes: The actual idea is inspired by the song itself, so I decided it should be included into the SCP itself. The song itself sounds like a drug trip, and is considered creepy by some of my friends, so it seemed like a good idea to state it as a hallucinogenic SCP. Then it made more sense to have a pelican involved as the actual object, and a stuffed animal seemed like a fair play. It's normally a rather insignificant and everyday object that is changed by it's anomalous properties. I'm also looking for critiques to make the proposal/other proposals in the future better, not just the greenlight to make it an article.

item.title: My First Idea
item.description: Idea for an SCP article involving a humanoid entity made of metal
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

  • Humanoid entity with metallic skin
  • Reaction to people it encounters depends on their morality

Central Narrative: The SCP was once a normal human woman, possibly someone who worked for the Foundation, who was badly mutilated and killed in an accident that may or may not have involved another SCP. A few hours after her death, her body spontaneously reanimated and morphed into a large humanoid entity with skin that appeared to be made of metal. It was then taken into Foundation custody, where its abilities and personality became apparent during testing.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The SCP's gimmick is that it reacts differently to people it encounters depending on what it perceives their morality to be. Upon looking at a person it encounters, it can instantly gauge their morality, though the standards by which it defines good and evil aren't entirely understood. If it sees someone as not being evil, it will typically ignore them, but if it sees someone as evil, it experiences a dramatic increase in aggression, often attempting to kill them.

Additional Notes: If anyone has any feedback or suggestions as to how I could elaborate upon or improve the idea, I'd be glad to hear it. If the idea is too similar to an existing SCP, please let me know, since I might not be aware since there are so many SCPs.

item.title: The Moloch Mentality
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Grigori Karpin and Ralliston for their crit. Site-36 is basically community property, and the community ought to do more with it.

SCPs obliquely referenced:

SCP-089: Tophet by spikebrennan
SCP-475: Soap of a Pope by mlister and Tarage
SCP-4263: Gemini by Tanhony

Tales directly referenced:

Empire of Dirt (Part 3 by Djoric
GRANT REQUEST FOR UTILIZATION OF ELECTRO-THAUMIC COMPUTERS TO PERFORM EXORCISMS OF DEMONIC ENTITIES by GreenWolf

item.title: Fungal Colony
item.description: Aliens come in all shapes and sizes
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: An alien fungus lands in an isolated town, infecting the surrounding plants as well as many of the mammals, birds, and a portion of the people turning the latter into violent cannibals. At first it seems like a standard zombie scenario but the fungus is in fact sentient and sapient and the violent ones were the result of an accident when they first arrived.

Central Narrative: The article begins by showing the SCP as a typical zombie pathogen then goes into some detail about their extraterrestrial origins before shifting to the Foundation discovering crude notes leading to the house of a serial killer with the implication that not all those infected are violent and those that are were the result of said killer being one of the first infected and his tendencies along with the fungi having never encountered intelligent life, creating a negative reaction. It then ends with their pleas to return to meteorite (which is actually an unconventional generation ship) so they can seek out a new home.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: A twist on the typical zombie outbreak along with some exploration about the unusual form alien life might take and how difficult it may be to communicate.

Additional Notes: May get slightly technical due to some real life parasite biology I'd like to integrate.

item.title: Does Spicy Crust Pizza have an email address?
item.description: Just an in-universe question.
item.content:encoded: I'm making a skip and it involves the Foundation Front company, Spicy Crust Pizza. I'm wondering if that company has an official email in-universe. If there is one, do you know what the name of it is?

item.title: (SEEKING GREENLIGHT) an existential philosophy
item.description: SCP philosophy
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes. got crit in IRC

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Philosophical

Elevator Pitch: A philosophy that makes SCP personnel realize that they are story characters

Central Narrative: The SCP foundation will discover that they are not real and suppress this information from not only the world but also themselves

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I'm going to have not only that personnel realize they are story characters but also they will realize the reader is there

Additional Notes: any critique or feedback welcome, I know this is a complex idea but i'm sure that I can run away with the idea

item.title: A REALLY underrated podcast
item.description: This really bugs me
item.content:encoded:
I found this podcast not that long ago but the videos in the podcast have really good discussions in them and really good editing. Yes the podcast/channel IS new but it still bothers me so go take a look at it if you don't like it leave if ya like it sub and show the support this man needs
Here's the link to the channel:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC3-tiMJw4PmyfQR-PlrGvZg

item.title: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded:
How is it even possible that SCPs are actually even allowed for publication? I have only ever seen one green light go out throughout the tens of ideas I have over viewed.

Were the first couple thousand SCPs published before the publication became so strict?

I'm just not sure if people aren't creative enough, don't think it through enough, or if the moderators are too unwilling to give it a try.

item.title: SCP Idea: Cogito, Ergo Sum
item.description: A class taught on the "Philosophy of Existence"
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Psychological

Page Layout (Optional): Interview articles, "Sit-in Classes"

Elevator Pitch: Students who want college credit attend a class taught by an unusual professor.

Central Narrative: Letters from "South Euton University" are sent out to high school and college students who want college credit. The class being taught is labeled "Philosophy of Existence," and is taught by Professor Phillip Oliver Zofia. The classroom has an unusual setup, with strange posters and maps that have countries improperly labeled. Above the whiteboard is a large poster, upon which is written, "Cogito, Ergo Sum." The professor himself is a man described as "gaunt," "faint," even "ethereal."

+ show block
– hide block

The class begins with the professor providing the students with an initial quiz. The questions are very easy (2+2=?), but the students fail when they provide standard answers. The professor then proceeds to teach the class, telling them blatantly incorrect answers, and then testing them on the incorrect knowledge. Those that refuse to answer "properly" are given personal instruction by Dr. Zofia. The sessions apparently take half an hour, though one student describes it feeling as though she was in there for days (vague as to whether she was or not). Invariably, the students start giving him the answers he wants, whether through exasperation or actually starting to buy into his teaching. They become increasingly suggestible as this goes on. Simultaneously, Dr. Zofia grows stronger, fuller, more substantial.

The final test consists of a piece of paper, asking, "Cogito, ergo sum. Concur?" There is also a line where the students write their names, which they invariably do. At the conclusion of the test, the students disappear, while Dr. Zofia seems to become completely rejuvenated. The SCP agent monitoring this situation (implied that Zofia is amused by their presence and is toying with them) offers them a letter and an invitation to join them next semester, and then is ushered out.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Aside from the oddities of the classroom, the central question, "I think, therefore I am," is explored. If one stops thinking, do they really exist? And if one's thoughts supercede another's, does that not make them more you, than you? This is also meant to be mild commentary on the education system caring more about correct answers than understanding material.

Additional Notes:

item.title: SCP Idea: Cogito, Ergo Sum
item.description: A class taught on the "Philosophy of Existence"
item.content:encoded: Duplicate, posting was acting weird.

item.title: My Small Idea
item.content:encoded: My idea for an SCP is a very strange painting of a cityscape that when viewed for an extended period of time they begin to look like the people shown in the picture, until they dissapear. And then appear in the picture a week later

item.title: SCP-5468
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -13.

If you are not the author and you want to rewrite this article, you may reply to this post asking for the opportunity to do so. Please obtain permission from the author (or the Rewrite Team if this article is older than 6 months) and make sure you copy the page source to your sandbox. Please do not reply to this post for any other reason unless you are staff.

item.title: SClippy!
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately we already have an SCP (joke essay, really) like this that executes this premise much better. -1

item.title: SCP Idea - A living Zebra
item.description: A horse that will survive, somehow
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: It's a zebra that doesn't die. It doesn't have supernatural resilience, but circumstance always works out in such a way that it survives. If a roof collapses, the portion of roof over the zebra will turn out to be still standing.

Central Narrative: The foundation realizes it's probably a bad idea to fuck with something when it doesn't have to
-The concept is introduced ("lucky zebra"),
-A list of relevant extraordinary circumstances is given
-The last example is enumerated upon:
-Toxins are administered to the horse
-The test is aborted when it almost dies
-It is concluded the horse "accepted" the toxins because we would save it
-Dr.McGuffin proposes injecting it and not saving it
-Because it's just a Zebra, the only way it could survive is by something preventing the staff from injecting toxins
-Testing is suspended

Hook/Attention-Grabber: It's a probability warper that warps around the idea of being a normal zebra. Although it poses no risk of breaching containment, because it's a normal zebra, containment will be breached if that it what's necessary to keep it alive.

Additional Notes: It's written in the vein of many older SCPs: very brief, and more "weird with dangerous implications" than scary

item.title: SCP Cosplay suggestions
item.content:encoded:
So in my country there is no Halloween but a holiday later in the year called Yom Kipur (יום כיפור)
and at that holiday you dress up as anything you want and generally celebrate in school, work, parties etc… So for this year i wanted to cosplay as a humanoid SCP of course but I don't know which one. Send me suggestions and I will pick the most interesting ones. Thanks!

item.title: They Lie In The Shadows (Seeking Greenlight)
item.description: A routine mission turns into a life or death escape attempt
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Layout: SCP article

Genre: Horror

Page Layout: The first part is a description of the actual SCP creatures. Then it transitions to the audio logs from the mission made by its commander.

Elevator pitch: A city is revealed to be overrun by tall and abnormally skinny creatures with long fingers, an almost circular head, and an abnormally large mouth. The creatures also originated from some kind of fungus growth, which results in their outer appearance being unusual bumpy and allows them to reproduce at an astonishing speed. They can climb up and down 90-degree surfaces with ease and can distort their bodies in ways that shouldn’t be possible. The creatures appear to be blind but possess some kind of echolocation as they will make a clicking noise when on the hunt. The creatures seem almost immune to bullets although enough bullets will kill them. The creatures do have one major weakness though, light. It appears that any kind of light burns the creature’s skin and they will always try to avoid it.

Central Narrative: The foundation gets word of multiple disappearances along with reported sightings of humanoid figures in [redacted], so just to be safe, they check it out. But soon discover that the city has been destroyed by an unknown entity or entities. After this discovery, a task force is sent to find out what happened, but the mission goes from a simple search and report, to a living nightmare, as the deeper they go the more unsettling it gets. Soon many on the task force have feelings of being watched and see shapes in the corner of their eyes. Yet they are told to continue and ignore these fears. That is until it turns to night and these creatures come out of the shadows. The whole operation then turns into a life or death escape attempt.

Hook/Attention Grabber: The hook is the Task force’s journey throughout the city that later turns into a life or death escape attempt

Additional Notes: If you have any critiques or ideas for this I would really like to know. whether you think it's bad or good I don't really care I just want to know what people think.

item.title: The Power of The Atom -revised
item.description: Multidimensional Facility with a powerful entity trapped inside
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Mystery, Horror, Drama

Elevator pitch and central narrative
Elevator pitch and central narrative

Elevator Pitch: Foundation operatives discover a distress signal similar to that of Foundation Site-17, however Site-17 is not anywhere near the distress signal's origination. A MTF unit is deployed at origination point to discover a massive sinkhole going approximately 300 meters into the ground. The MTF repel down the shaft to find it breaches the roof of an identical reconstruction of Site-17.

The MTF unit discover dozens of burned corpses located around the facility, however no SCP items contained at that site can be found. Data logs found in a computer in the site talk about some sort of nuclear apocalypse, the last day recorded being June 15th, 2077. The unit make their way toward the generator room of the facility in an attempt to restore power when they find a child sitting in one of the hallways. The MTF ask the child what happened and why he was here. The child described: "My daddy drove us here… he is gone now. The monster sucked the life out of him. Are you here to kill the monster? I trapped it, let me show you where!" The MTF follow the child to the generator room.

When they reach the room, they find a high tech electromagnetic fence surrounding the generators and a large humanoid lifeform trapped on the fence. One of the MTF decided to turn off the fence with the control panel on it. The entity fell to the floor with a bang. It rose from the ground and suddenly a large explosion came from the entity causing everything within the room to be destroyed, but not the entity.

Central Narrative: The lifeform is the reason the facility exists. The facility is from another reality and somehow got to this reality. In another reality, a nuclear war occurred causing most of humanity to be wiped out, except those in the facility. They were supposed to escape but the creature stopped them. The creature seems to be able to cause great damage to areas around it. In interviews, the creature claims to be the one that caused the nuclear disaster in the alternate reality.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The creature is a reality bender of immense proportions, with the capability to cause atomic destruction with a thought. It could also be used as a weapon against other groups… or SCPs.

Additional Notes:

The Facility and the entity will be split into 2 different SCPs.

I am planning to add many interviews, exploration logs, and crosstests with other SCPs.

item.title: Social Suicide: SCP Idea
item.description: An object that erases anyone who comes in contact with it from the memories of anybody that has ever met them.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: No

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): N/A (Yet)

Elevator Pitch: If you come in contact with the object, all memories of you will be erased from everyone you ever met.

Central Narrative: The central narrative focuses on 4 instances of the use of the objects and how this affected the lives of the victims.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: After testing, the foundation finds files on high profile personnel that nobody has ever heard of meaning that the object has been found before and been used as a "weapon" against government officials, civilians, and even other SCPs in the Foundation.

Additional Notes: I'm playing around with the idea that the object could be used for taking high priority persons and SCPs since it would be a quick an easy way to make someone disappear without having to give amnestics to a large number of people. I'm also thinking of making the object a small eraser.

item.title: (Seeking Greenlight) Just a normal old mirror.
item.description: Seeking greenlight
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Horror (kinda?)

Page Layout (Optional): Just a regular SCP layout with 1 addendum and 3 expeditions

Elevator Pitch: This SCP is essentially a mirror from an unknown company that, when people enter the reflection radius of it, it "sucks" them into a mirror dimension, where creepy stuff happens.

Central Narrative: It revolves around 3 expedition logs in which more about the world is developed, including wierd entities who hate outsiders and can do some kind of wierd "magic"

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The "other dimension" idea is done quite a bit, I agree, but I feel like it could be decently unique.

Additional Notes: I feel like this is really bad so I agree with every single bit of harsh criticism

item.title: Changing wikidot username
item.description: And consequences thereof.
item.content:encoded: My username is sort of unwieldy. I’ve begun shortening it to “Koth” in chat and am thinking of changing my actual username, but I’m worried about the consequences of such. Will places where my name is hyperlinked (Kothardarastrix) automatically change to the new name, or will the links break or something? What about places where it isn’t, like old news pages? Will I need to go back and change those, will they change automatically, will someone else do it, or is not worth worrying about old news pages anyway? Are there any other advantages, disadvantages, or consequences of changing my username that I might not be aware of?

item.title: Sandbox Bug
item.description: Sandbox isn't accepting me as a member, even though I've been accepted.
item.content:encoded: I attempted to access the sandbox to do a draft on an SCP, not to post, just to get some practice in writing one. However, it said the page I'm trying to access does not exist, and brought me to a page that said only "create page". However, when I clicked on it, it said only site administrators, mods, etc. may create a sandbox page. Does anybody know how to fix this?

item.title: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.
item.description: Excuse me, but can somebody give me crits or greenlight on this thread?
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Humor/Emotional

Page Layout: Classic

Elevator Pitch: A pitched black humanoid-entity with reality-altering properties, dressed up in a mafia's suit of the late 19s, however, with the mind of a "naughty scapegoat" and the soul of a benevolent trickster who was formerly a man named Michael Malloy.

Central Narrative:

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP is a helpful and relief trickster who the Foundation described as a "suicidal idiot". Michael is very humorous as he always smiles around with others in the facility. Michael was discovered when he attempted to persuade a female Foundation researcher to become his girlfriend while walking her way home. As a result, he was brought back and held in Foundation custody afterward. According to what it is known, Michael states that he was brought back to life by a "transparent ghost" after being murdered by a group of violent scammers that pretends to be his new only best friends. Michael claims that he was bestowed with a power named "Anti-God," but it is unknown of its effects to all, including Michael. The Foundation temporarily presume it is the name of its reality-bending properties. For times being held in Foundation custody, Michael has proven to be extremely reckless and puts his life on the line to save others for testing to see if everything in the world truly can't kill him. Still, sometimes he does this for no reason. This draws confusion to the Foundation as they couldn't understand his nature. However, despite all these, a term is known as "The ones that have the brightest smiles, and the loudest laughs have the most painful past." Michael wasn't actually a person that lived a good life when he was still a human. He never had friends. He was alone. He lost his job. His father died in the war, and his mother died due to sickness right in-front of him when he was a child. Michael doesn't want anyone to experience the same way that he had, which is why he helped them for no reason. He feared to be reminded of his sad past.


Hook/Attention-Grabber: Overall, the story is about a tragic anomaly that smiles endlessly while hiding what it really feels inside of its heart. (In short, it is like wearing a mask to cover your identity.)

Additional Notes: This SCP was inspired by The Mask (1994). The Draft is still incomplete. Yeah, his ways of acting are like comedy characters from the late and mid 19s. If you wonder why the title is written as "Sinister Fedora", then it was because of his appearance. The entity's appearance drives sinister chills down to your spines.

item.title: [Seeking Greenlight] SCP Concept - The Drain Cover
item.description: A floor drain cover which fills the room with water until a victim drowns.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Horror

Page Layout (Optional): N/A (Suggestions Needed)

Elevator Pitch:

  • A floor drain cover that constantly emits a gurgling sound typical in a drain clogged with semi-permeable debris.
  • Upon inspection, it will begin to overflow and fill the room with dark, murky water until the victim drowns.
  • The water overflowing from the drain cover is chemically identical, but significantly less dense than normal water. This makes it very difficult to swim or float in.
  • The drain cover will only begin to overflow if the room is small and closed.
  • All effects only occur when the drain cover is appropriately fixed over a floor drain.

Central Narrative: The SCP was discovered at a Oklahoma public swimming pool showering facility following renovations that made the room a closed area, previously separated with curtains, leading to the death of 2 young boys and their father.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The theme is the fear of drowning and the disgust of a drain overflowing with dirty water.

Additional Notes:

  • I want the drain cover to have some psychological abilities, such as:
      • Making victims believe all exits are locked or otherwise broken.
      • Making victims believe the water is unbearably cold.
      • Causing uncontrollable panic when the overflowing begins.

All feedback and criticism is wanted. I am very new to the site and have been thinking carefully about ideas for weeks now. I hope this idea is interesting to readers, or more accurately, that it can become interesting.

item.title: Editi page without bumping?
item.content:encoded: Is there a way to edit a forum page without it moving up to the top of the list? I noticed a silly grammatical error shortly after posting and want to fix it without seeming like I am trying to bump my page. I feel like the answer is no; but figure it's worth asking.

item.title: My Idea
item.content:encoded:
So I had this idea for an SCP and wanted to know if it would do well;

The Idea is a very bizarre painting of a cityscape in a strange abstract style, and there are people in it. If you look at the Painting you body begins to morph into the style in the painting until you dissapear and appear in the painting, becoming another of the paintings people.

Just wanted some thoughts

item.title: SCP-5978
item.content:encoded: So it's a grater? And the grater makes you grate yourself?

item.title: The Artifact - SCP Concept
item.description: An idea for a new SCP seeking greenlight
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Horror/Suspense

Page Layout: Apart from the standard description and containment procedures, there will be a log of the containment breach of the anomaly following an agent attempting to survive and escape. The log will contain several videos and audio files of the incident, not just text logs, with the goal of adding to the horror and suspense.

Elevator Pitch: It will be an anomalous disease that causes every infected organism to become a "low poly model" of themselves, their "textures" to have JPEG artifacts, and their voice to have compression artifacts. Additionally, the infected individual is perceived like a bad CGI, staying at the same place relative to the observer's eyes. The disease is spread through touch or prolonged exposure.

Central Narrative: The main part will be the log of the Site-195 containment breach, following an agent attempting to first contain the SCP, and then escape the site. The log will be accompanied by several pictures, and audio and video recordings from the agent's camera. The story itself will be about the agent. At first, they and their fellow agents will try to contain the breach, but as the site gets more and more overrun, it soon become obvious that they'll fail. The agent gets in contact with an off site administrator, asking what to do, and they decide that they will detonate the site self destruct mechanism, as the site is built only to contain this specific anomaly. But before the detonation, the administrator requires more data in case there will be another outbreak, so the agent grabs recording equipment and starts following the administrator's tasks until they'll let them run away. In the end, the administrator decides that it's too much of a risk to let the agent out, as he might already be infected, and detonates the self destruct mechanism with the agent still inside.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The main attention grabber is the log. Two parts of the log are attention grabs. First is the story, which will be a suspense story. The other is the media captured from the agent's recording equipment. The main thing that differentiates the article will be the inclusion of actual videos and not just of transcriptions.

Additional Notes: I mainly want feedback on the idea, as I believe in my ability to create low poly 3d models and put them on pictures, and in audio editing.

item.title: SCP-4273
item.content:encoded: Zalgo text is a bit too late 00's for my taste, fella. -1

item.title: SCP-4273 REAL Files
item.content:encoded: You misspelt Apollyon.

item.title: EAS Alerts that predict disaster.
item.description: In essence the EAS warning system will on occasion show events like hurricane and tornado warnings before they happen.Disaster can also be anomalous in nature.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes.

PageType: Scp Article.

ElevatorPitch: There is a radio station at a undisclosed location within North America that when active sends out a broadcast bi-monthly of upcoming events, most frequently natural disasters before they occur.However on rare occasions it will send out a alert for an upcoming Scp or containment breach.It will state the day and area it will occur in and what protocols to take in order to survive or even recontain if possible.

CentralNarrative: The Foundation goes through a ethical dilemma of sort over whether or not to trust the information given by the alerts.However when the information provided proves to be correct and vital for minimizing casualties they slowly begin to trust and utilize it.The advice for recontainment is in particular valuable for Scps whose properties are not yet fully understood.

Hook: It will be revealed later after discovering the origins of this anomaly to be that of the chaos insurgency however that it wasn’t predicting these events, it was causing them and by keeping the signal active in order to save lives they have accidentally killed quite a few people.It’s true nature in essence is that it’s warnings are a self fulfilling prophecy.

item.title: Favorite Hobbies before covid-19?
item.content:encoded: Before this stinky virus what did you like to do in public spaces and in your home? except for sitting and writing SCP's all-day what did you like to do?

item.title: The barman
item.description: My idea for an scp sounds simple and maybe a bit siply
item.content:encoded:
Scp n/a was found at a bar in massachusets at a bar under the name of [REDACTED] at 2/3/1988 the entity appears to be a middle aged caucasian male who does not seem to have the ability or to the least extent unwilling to feel happiness.

The foundation was called in after reports of another bar patron claimed he owned a business that did not currenrly exist at the time until it was mentiones to scp n/a this business and its main building did not seem to be anomolous besides how they were created they have been deignated scp n/a-1 anything made into reality by scp n/a will be similarly designated.

Another thing to note about scp n/a is that it does not seem to age ever since its capture its entire bodily structure did not age even though it was captured more than 2 decades ago. Another anomolous property of scp n/a is that it also does not seem to have its reality bending powers active if a human atleast at the legal drinking age of the country it is held in goes within 20 feet of the entity, and it also seems to only be able to bend reality to make things true if what it says or what someone says to it is untrue and it must be impaired by some form of alchahol.

Though at first foundation researchers thought they could deprive scp n/a of alchahol it seemed to materialize a flask of whiskey from its pocket though the entity had no signs of posessing this flask beforehand with the fact that when impaired it could change reality in any way as long as it was currently untrue it was placed in heavy humanoid containment in site [REDACTED] and no personell under level 4 acess are allowed to enter the contaibmebt cell of or in any way interact with scp n/a and with all of these things in mind scp n/a was classified as euclid class by the foundation.

Containment Procedures

Scp n/a is to be kept in a 20ftX20ft humanoid containment cell and is to be fed 3 times per day by a automated robotics vehicle and if humans enter its cells only exact truths are to be spoken to scp n/a if communication is necessary unless the 05 council approves of doing otherwise along with this it is recommended to interupt scp n/a if it is about to tell a lie as to stop it from altering reality if a human is within 20 feet of its cells.

the furnishings of scp1 n/a's cell is to be upkept as the foundation does not know the full extent if the entities power and does not wish to displease it if possible. With the overall power of scp n/a it is urged that containment procedures are always followed as under the wrong circumstances an XK class scenario could occur due to scp n/a.

End Document

item.title: Any attempt to use this product in a manner inconsistent with it's labeling...
item.description: An idea for an ever-changing bottle of cleaning supplies
item.content:encoded:
An idea for an ever-changing bottle of cleaning supplies that could be deadly if used incorrectly…

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

It is a safe-class object.
It manifests as a random bottle of cleaning supplies consistent with several non-anomolous brands of cleaning supplies.
It causes horrible injuries to people who use the bottle's contents for any purpose other than cleaning.
Injuries range from lacerations to third-degree burns on your face.
Can change form when unobserved for a period of ██ to █ hours

Safe
Central Narrative:
In a foundation sweep of a anomalous materials distribution operation in █████, ██████. The object was found in a janitorial closet in the building where the operations were taking place. The object in question was at the time taking the form of a bottle of clorox-brand bleach. On the return to site-██ It had changed into the form of a bottle of windex. It's dangerous properties were discovered when during a test of it's chemical composition the researcher spontaneously combusted and suffered lacerations. Further tests concluded that the object was only dangerous when being used in a way the object's label did not support.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:
An everyday object that will never hurt you if you use it right, But if you dare step out of line, A painful punishment awaits.

item.title: New Writer, New SCP Idea
item.description: Hi, my name is Teddy and I've had this idea for an SCP for awhile now
item.content:encoded:
Hi, my name is Teddy and I've had this idea for an SCP for awhile now, and I really want to get people's input on it.

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre(s): Horror, Drama, etc

Page Layout: I plan on using the standard sort of SCP formatting that I've seen for most other articles use.

Elevator Pitch: My main idea is I want to create an SCP that plays on my somewhat odd fear of being kidnapped and subsequently brainwashed/conditioned to be someone else's family member, primarily their child. The SCP in question appears to most individuals as their most beloved parental figure in life (parent, older sibling, etc), but to people who have never had any sort of positive role model, the SCP appears in her true form, which resembles a humanoid insect. Upon the SCP meeting such a person, she will pull them into a hug, and after five minutes of being embraced, the victim is physically and mentally regressed to the state of an infant, whom the SCP will then raise as it's own until the victim reaches adulthood.

The SCP in question actually isn't secretly eating the kids or something like that, but she’s still scary in that what she’s doing is meant to be beneficial to the victim, at least from her perspective. I'm not sure if I'm expressing this very well here, but what makes her scary is how nice she is, in that she could give you a better childhood, but at the cost of who you’ve become.

+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative: Like I've kinda already hinted at, the theme here is one of brainwashing, and how the SCP clearly believes she’s doing the right thing by reraising the individuals she chooses to “adopt”, but she does so (almost always) without consent.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I feel like while kidnapping and child abuse are major themes in a lot of horror stories, nothing I've seen quite captures the fear of seemingly being “rescued” by your abductor. It wasn't the victim's decision to start over, but now that they have, is it moral to just give in and accept your new life, or to fight back? Are you somehow now responsible for your abduction if you accept it? Are you selfish for fighting back when you've been shown almost nothing but love and affection? These are the sorts of questions I want to present to the reader.

Additional Notes: I worry I'll make the SCP too Mary Sue-like, so I would appreciate some advice here!

All criticism is welcome here, and I'd love to get some advice from those of you with more experience. Again, I'm a newbie here, but I've been doing creative writing since about 2013, so I like to think I'm not going into this completely blind.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. Also, please note the Ideas Critique required reading wordcount limit; your post is about 200 words over. Please address this before seeking further feedback. ~Zyn

item.title: SCP Idea Seeking Greenlight - Mayan Blues
item.description: How did the Mayan's disappear seemingly "into the blue"?
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Historical-Fiction

Elevator Pitch:
SCP is a pigment of "Mayan Blue" color developed and mass produced in the early 1900s by a now defunct chemical company. A chemical mystery for many years, the company appears to have uncovered a long lost technique dating back to the Mayan's that creates the pigment, which carries unexpected anomalous properties. These properties carry over to any object painted or colored with the pigment. Any surfaces colored with the blue will act as a sort of dimensional rift, with objects able to pass through into it. While most objects do not have enough painted surface area to pose major physical risk, if an individual were to pass through completely, they would find themselves in another dimension. In particular, a dimension that seems to largely be the inspiration for much of Mesoamerican mythology; populated with many entities from those myths.The land has also been settled long ago by none other than the Mayans themselves, a people who history recounts as seemingly having disappeared near the decline of their civilization.

Show More
Show Less

Central Narrative:
For years, the foundation has contained a number of the pigmented objects and has largely erased the existence of the pigment from record. Over time, the anomalous affects have faded from all objects large enough to pass through and connection with the other world has been lost; all attempts to recreate the pigment have proved futile. That is until in the late 1900's a university researcher and avid collector of "rare colors" stumbles across an un-found document on the pigment and attempts to recreate it thinking the pigment had simply been lost to the ages. The researcher eventually succeeds and unwittingly stumbles into the other world after painting his office wall. The incident ends up on the desk of the Foundation and an expedition into the rift is launched with the hopes of retrieving the professor so that they may acquire his knowledge on the creation of the pigment.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:
The search-and-rescue exploration into an alternate dimension housing the not only living myths but a long thought "deceased" civilization.

Additional Notes: While I have done quite some research for the concept, I am by no means an expert on the history of pigments nor on Mesoamerican history/culture. If anyone happens to be more well versed on either subject, your feedback is very much welcome.

item.title: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)
item.description: A sword AND a ballerina... that turns its owner's life into hell.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Drama, Narrative-driven.

Page Layout (Optional): As other narrative-driven SCPs, the page will have different iterations of the SCP article.

Elevator Pitch: A gladius that can transform into a ballet dancing woman-like fighting automaton. It has a host which, when killed, pass the "hosting" to their murder. If the owner was killed in an unintentional way or to a non-humanoid killer, the SCP will become the protector of its host until it is defeated. SCP is looking to be a "Perfect Weapon" for a "Perfect Warrior", so it will defend its host from killing attempts turning into its automaton form. Also it will keep and share every past hosts' skills that can be used on warfare and some memories, turning the actual host more proficient as a combatant. Even an owner's seemingly useless proficiencies, as a past owner’s ballet dancing dexterity is weaponized by the SCP, turned into a fighting style.

Central Narrative: The narrative is more centred on the host, the "SCP-XXXX-A". They'll be an unintentional host as they defeated the SCP while retrieving it. As the narrative starts, they are just an common loyal field agent that was in the wrong place at the wrong time. This host swiftly will discover SCP “memory flash drive" powers and will be turned into the subject of an mnestic experiment to discover the SCP memories. First experiments are normal and very positive, revealing previous owners and how the SCP got its abilities and the abilities it gives to the host. As it continues, the hosts start presenting PTSD signals by living another person's memories even out of the experiments, mainly killings and war memories. Psychological, therapeutic and psychiatric accompaniments start getting more and more necessary. Amnestics are tried, but as the SCP “reloads” the memories every time they’re erased, it’s worthless. As more accidents took place, the experiments ceased. Main directive becomes containing both SCP and its host in a chamber as the host’s hyperarousal episodes turn the SCP violent. The Foundation have to repeatedly contain the host from killing themself as their psychological condition is going worse.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The host's events are more connected to the Foundation's experiments and protocols than the SCP itself as the mnestics paved the way for this to happen. As the Foundation have to deal with the results of their actions, they tries to keep the host alive in their bad psychological state.

Additional Notes: I want to know if you think the SCP is weak for being focused on its "owner''. Also, I want to know if the narratives are interesting or if in its anachronic file-based narrative the story will become boring. I'll try to keep with the "Cold, not Cruel" and "Flawed, not Fools" all the time, but I need more critics about it.

item.title: Tagging Workbench
item.content:encoded: Reserved.

item.title: Seeking Greenlight: The Indiscernible Infection
item.description: I am new to the site and am trying to get some help as well as get my concept greenlit.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

  • Untraceable infection.
  • Infection spreads by being in close proximity to an infected individual.
  • Infected individuals do not retain the knowledge of their past life.
  • Infected individuals speak an unknown language.
  • Infected individuals ignore people that are not infected.
  • Individuals suffering from amnesia can not be infected.

Central Narrative: [none]

Hook/Attention-Grabber: [The Infection is untraceable and can not be found within tissue samples of an infected individual. An infected individual only has to be within a specific radius to be infected. The infection cannot infect individuals suffering from amnesia.]

item.title: Bicephalic(SEEKING GREENLIGHTS)
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights:Yes

Page Type:SCP

Elevator Pitch:A humanoid with a human body, except the skin area covering the collarbone and above has been replaced with teil scales. Instead of a human head, this SCP has two snake like heads, both about 20.32 centimeters (8 inches) long. They both have a set of blue eyes. However, the 20.32 inches is just the minimum. This humanoid creature may extend the length of its necks, up to 91.44 centimeters(36 inches) long. These heads are 12.7 centimeteres (5 inches) in width, so any opening leading out of its containment with a width equal or bigger than 12.7 centimeters is prohibited. Since it isn't that dangerous and has free will, this object has obtained the classification of Euclid.

Central Narrative:A human like being, with two reptilian heads and necks, complete with scales. These necks and heads are complete with scales. Despite stretching out and biting, this SCP serves no real risk of death.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:This SCP is very loosely based off of snakeneck turtles with bicephaly, and the idea or the source materials may attract in readers.

item.title: Learn To Live
item.content:encoded:
This is my Art Exchange gift to Uncle Nicolini!

holiday cheer…

Thank you to DarkStuff for critiquing and letting me give this extra gift to Nico. I kinda wish it didn't break the canon of What a Wonderful World so much, because I think I wrote this pretty well considering the events in that canon and Fae's personality. But alas, it's alright, I had a lot of fun writing it and I hope you like it too.

item.title: I would like to speak to your manager...
item.description: Idea for a SCP that turns people against wearing masks, social distancing, and harasses young retail workers...
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

Memetic hazard only affecting a certain amount of the population
Impossible to fully contain.
Multiple cognitohazardous vectors of contamination.
Instances of persons controlled by the cognitohazard can be cured with amnestics and released.
Accompanying the 2020 novel coronavirus pandemic.
Based off (But not a carbon copy of) The internet meme of "Karens".
Any text/video/images that the afflicted persons post on social media become cognitohazardous and could turn more people into "Karens/darren".
Keter-class.
Makes individuals karen, basically.

Central Narrative:
This SCP appeared online at a redacted date and affected a redacted amount of individuals with a cognitohazard that turns people into rude mask-hating social distancing protesters. The foundation has several instances of people affected by the cognitohazard in containment for study. And they have breached containment a redacted amount of times. Foundation webcrawlers must constantly work to erase cognitohazardous posts on social media. A redacted number of mobile task forces are
Working to find and break up cognitohazardous gatherings of the SCP affected individuals. Several interviews with cognitohazardous individuals are accompanying the scp report.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:
Scp can be observed in public with a cover story disguising it as a mental illness instead of an anomaly.

In hindsight this is an awful idea. But if you guys can figure out a way for me to salvage it youre welcome to try.

item.title: SCP seeking greenlight- Ancient Nautiloids
item.description: Seeking to greenlight my SCP idea
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: 4 unseen ancient lineages of nautiloids are found around the Atlantic Ocean. Each one is drastically different from the other. Though interact with each other rarely, on an instance of each lineage will head for the Bermuda Triangle for a deathmatch for fertility rights.

+ Central
- Central

Central Narrative: Four undiscovered nautiloids species were discovered fighting in the middle of the Bermuda Triangle by an international oceanography survey. They were later discovered in different parts of the Atlantics with little overlap in population overlap.

Every 10 years, a ritual of fertility will be held by each instance of SCP-XXXX at the center of the Bermuda Triangle. The ritual ends until the last instance is alive. The sole survivor of the ritual, for example, an instance of SCP-XXXX-B, will have a population boom of three years for its instance.

+ Hook
- Hook

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Humans know more of what’s above them than what’s below the deep oceans. The oceans are scary indeed. About 90-97% of the ocean is still undiscovered. The ocean is the last frontier to travel on this planet. Many creatures are left undiscovered to this day.

Additional Notes: Draft is complete. Sketches for SCP instances are finished

item.title: The Modification Shop
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: No, I would like feedback.

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: The Modification Shop is a shop that will keep on growing from only Human Modification to cars, guns, and more. The shop is run by a machine and the shop has a invisible barrier that will not allow you to leave if have any not paid for items on you or if you have not paid yet. The things you buy from the shop are modifications that will be used as modules for the system when the subject is placed inside a tube, one of the modules makes the space of the module capacity bigger. It starts at 3 and the module will use one space and will add 3 spaces. The shop will make its own prices as you buy the items and it must be paid in full within 24 hours or the people in the shop at that time will be released and the shop will close for 1 week. The shop earns a safe class as it will do no harm.

Central Narrative: The shop is an ever-growing shop without limits. You buy modules that make modifications to the object you put in the modification room. The shop is run by a robot and if you don’t pay or try to leave with an unpaid item a force will not allow the subject to leave the shop. The prices of the shop's items can be bargained and the foundation usually uses it to generate their military or D-Class. They also are still testing the capabilities of the machine because of the fact it keeps on growing, and that there is an Everything-Bank, The Everything-Bank is a machine that gives points for things you put in depending on the value. There is a Modification card where you store the points, and as in the name, it can eat everything so the foundation uses it as their Rubbish bin while getting free points.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Need help with this.

Additional Notes:

item.title: Mr Wendo SCP idea
item.description: (Green light?) this is an SCP idea that is original to me
item.content:encoded:
(Green light?) this is an SCP idea that is original to me, little to no inspiration from other scp’s used, check it out? Give feedback?? Keep in mind this is my first written SCP

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

I’ll be posting the sandbox page as it is a little too long to read in this post. I have written the SCP as SCP XXXX until I get a number for it or it gets approved.

http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/mr-wendo

Thanks!

item.title: Just trying to be cool again
item.description: An AWCY? format about the greenlight process
item.content:encoded:
So after spending a considerable amount of time in IRC chat, I've come to notice a few things. A lot of people are more concerned with getting their ideas greenlit as opposed to crafting a good story. This is not the case for everyone, let me just preface that, but I do see it happen a fair amount of times.

This thought process is really upsetting to me. Why should anyone else's opinion be disregarded just because they can't give you a green light? Why would you think that your own thoughts are inadequate just because you can't give someone a greenlight? Doesn't make any sense to me, yet this seems to be the thought process for a lot of new authors on site. And many of them get discouraged and leave if their first idea doesn't work out.

Anyway, the preamble is over. I'm trying to convey all these emotions and such in the third tab of this sandbox. I'm really struggling with the subtlety of this one.

item.title: plantbirb scp idea
item.description: bird like plant that can mimic the aroma of other plants, said aroma have healing power
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: maybe

Page Type: Joke SCP

Genre : mystery

Page Layout (Optional): mostly as plot development, goin on what is the limits of the creature and who and why this was send to the foundation

Elevator Pitch:
mysterious sender
strange present
cute creature

Central Narrative:
it all started when a unknow person send a flower pod to the P.O box of a chief botanic of site 12, with a letter with no identification saying simple instructions to take care of the plant, just water twice per day and interact with it, as at first nothing seen abnormal or dangerous in nature and it was simply put in the garden

+ show block
– hide block

until it started revealing itself as a very shy and strange creature, with some "druid like powers" to simulate the smell of other plants and with that create some sort of healing and/or medicinal aura

but disaster strike when, for up until now unknow reasons it attacked a d class personnel, after the event said scp became even more wary about any other personnel around her that isnt her "owner", and thus it was move to said owner personnel quarters and until further testing its done, said owner may have to return home sooner in case her newfound pet leave her room and start lookin for her

Hook/Attention-Grabber:
mysterious creature, cute creature and mysterious sender of a very mysterious package
theres a lot of fun to be have with playing with this idea, did the misterious sender know it was a SCP?, will he send another letter, why did he send that to doctor [redacted/yet to think a name], will he send another gift in the future?

Additional Notes:
tho my intentions aren't the most serious, as my idea is mostly to rewrite a character i have in other format, while in doin so i am acting seriously, so i wanna follow the format as much as possible during this exercise for i respect this format and for the universe around it
so learning how to proper honor this will help me both doin this exercise with other "gift" i may send in the future as writing seriously


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

image of what the scp is suppose to look like

+ show block
– hide block

item.title: Multicolor EpiPen | Greenlight Requested
item.description: An autoinjector with multiple serums to choose from that change the user's emotions.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: An object with a mechanism resembling a combination of a multicolor pen and an epinephrine autoinjector. There are six different-colored tabs are near the top of the pen: weariness, lustfulness, happiness, sadness, irritableness, and frightfulness. A stadium-shaped cutout reveals a cylinder that swivels to give a name to the tab that has been pressed.

The needle must be injected into the deltoid and kept in for a minimum of 10 seconds. Cells from the serum follow the user's blood flow to the brain and exchange emotion-effecting proteins with the limbic system. Once complete, the cells follow the blood flow through the heart and back to the inserted needle to recycle the collected proteins. After use, the user should have the opposite emotion to what they had originally, and the object can still be reused later on.

+ Continued Information
- Hide Information

Central Narrative: The object was discovered by a man named ███ ████████. He discovered the object in his deceased father's home while preparing for an estate sale. ████████ pocketed it believing the object was an unused epinephrine autoinjector due to the instructions on the side of the pen that is "similar-looking from a distance". At a grocery store that afternoon ████████ sampled a cookie, only to undergo an allergic reaction to the nuts it contained. He retrieved the object from his pocket and pressed a random tab before injecting the liquid into the side of his thigh. After undergoing investigation, ████████ was revealed to have injected himself with the "irritableness" serum; when paramedics located him, he was incredibly frightful and tried to escape unsuccessfully. The local authorities handed the pen over to the Foundation after being unable to identify the substances within the object and overall deeming it "potentially malicious". ████████ has since recovered from the incident.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: This object could either be incredibly helpful or mind-breakingly dangerous. Before injection, you must press the tab of the emotion that you currently have, not the one you want. This is due to the cells being unable to retrieve incorrect proteins while still dropping off the ones it contains and causes an emotional clash.

Additional Notes:

  • I will not refer to the object as an EpiPen in the actual article. It is only in the title to help users get an idea of what the object is like before reading.
  • This is the first concept I've posted on this site. I apologize for any misused/missing lingo.

item.title: A small question
item.content:encoded:
So I have seen on quite a few discussion pages that the a few of the negative comments say

'Not very interesting'

Does anyone know why this is said, because I am curious as to whether this helpful to the author as one person could find it disinterestong but the next could find it absolutely fascinating.

item.title: Clinical Deficency
item.description: The SCP is a disease thing that spreads fast
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: Joke SCP

Genre: Comedy

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXX is a pathogen created by GOI Return to Basic, an anomalous group that wants to return humanity to a less scientifically advanced state. Its members are just old people that say back in my day a lot. The SCP is highly infectious, infecting the entirety of Site X within 24 hours. After approx. 2 hours of infection, the subject will begin to exhibit cold-like symptoms including nasal congestion, coughing and sneezing. After approx. 24 hours of infection, cold symptoms will disappear the subject will be unable to speak or write in a clinical tone.

Central Narrative: After the infection spread to all of Site X, the O5 council immediately declared it imperative for the foundation top priority to find a cure for SCP-XXX, unfortunately no cure exists nor can be made. However, it is discovered that 1% of the population is immune. The foundation frantically tries to find and train these people to be capable of turning casually spoken words into clinical SCP Foundation documents. To date, 10% of the foundation’s budget goes toward finding and training people immune to SCP-XXX

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The entire article is written in a non-clinical tone due to the outbreak of the SCP.

item.title: His "Favorite"
item.description: An SCP who is favored, yet hated by many.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Other (Mystery)

Page Layout (Optional): I would be using a very basic page layout, such as giving the readers the information about the SCP’s designated number, class, description, addendums, and any notes provided by foundation personnel.

- For reasons unknown, there are some SCP’s who refuse to interact with this specific D-Class member
- Anyone who interacts with them feels a sense of “calm” or “friendly familiarity”, it is unclear why
- After a more notable encounter with SCP-682, it has been speculated that there is someone or something keeping him from harm, hence being their “favorite”.

Elevator Pitch: A D-Class member who cannot be permanently harmed by other SCP’s or foundation staff members. Despite this, he can still be physically or mentally harmed if ever placed in extremely dire situations, however they can not killed.

+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative: D-Class member, Thomas Grey, is a 24 year old male of Caucasian decent with blonde hair and brown eyes. Before finding himself in the hands of the foundation, he was arrested, tried, and found guilty for the murder of two female roommates.

Thomas’ first assignment with the foundation was to interact with SCP-053, however, the moment he's placed in SCP-053's containment room he suddenly drops dead. Once the foundation removes the body, he’s brought back. They try this again and the results remain consistent. After this incident, he's given the orders to meet other SCP's for further testing, and miraculously he's able to complete or escape them unscathed, usually for reasons the foundation doesn't understand.

For example, Thomas’ placed in SCP-106's containment room without any instructions on how to properly interact with it, despite this, he somehow instinctively knows not to look away or blink, and stared at SCP-106 for exactly 3 hours before being safely removed. When asked about it, he doesn't know how to explain his actions. Personnel in charge of his Class-D district decide to take things a step further with more tests, but unfortunately, there is nothing remarkable or noticeable about his vitals or physical structure.

One of his most notable encounters is with SCP-096. He's given the photo to look at, but the subjects eyes role into the back of his head unwillingly before he could interact with it. No matter how many times he's ordered to look at it, he claims that he can't. Only after the photo is taken and properly contained does his eyes role back in place. When asked about his behavior once again, he claims to not know or understand how it happened, but does mention to have felt an overwhelming sense of dread the moment the envelope was handed to him.

Thomas is considered to be "favored" due to his unusual interaction with SCP-682. While interacting with the deadly lizard, he somehow manages to say something to it in Hebrew before the SCP has the chance to even think about attacking him. There’s a moment of pause before SCP-682 orders him to “Leave”, and Thomas quickly manages to somehow simply exit the room, despite the foundation previously locking him. When later translated, it is revealed that he told SCP-682 "You cannot harm me. Not while I have him on my side." Thomas doesn't recall ever saying that, nor does he speak Hebrew. SCP-682 is shown to be extremely annoyed, but will not say why. (Of course, neither wish to see one another ever again, but only time will tell…)

When interacting with foundation staff or other D-Class members, it's also been shown that others feel a sense of "calm" or "friendly familiarity" when around him, despite his criminal past. It's unclear if it's due to his casual approach and interactions with others, or something else. He's only shown true terror and uneasiness around SCP's that can or have the desire to take his life, that's when he usually performs (according to some) “miracles” to get out of said situations, almost as if someone’s guiding him.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Thomas Grey is a young man who is able to connect with foundation staff and a handful of SCP’s without harming himself or them. There’s nothing too outstanding about him, but if given the greenlight for his SCP, I would have him also work with the foundation as a sort of “therapist” in some ways, someone who staff can turn to simply for conversations sake, and/or to maintain a sense of ease from work pressure or other means. SCP’s will somehow also come to understand that there is something different about him compared to foundation staff or other D-Class members, and some will either have less desire, or no desire to harm him, for reasons not all will understand.

The foundation knows something, or someone, is looking out for Thomas, but it is unclear who or why him specifically is being cared for.

Additional Notes:
- Thomas is not a very emotive person, but does carry a strong sense of sympathy for foundation staff.
- He knows SCP-183, and it later is revealed they both went to middle school together before he was transferred for [REDACTED]. She remembers him, he doesn’t remember her.
- Thomas’ claims that he did not harm his two roommates, who were found to have died of asphyxiation in their apartment. Thomas was connected to their “murder” for reasons he doesn’t even quite understand.
- His foster mother was also found dead when he was young. She died of asphyxiation.
- When Thomas is put in a position where death only seems to be the possible outcome, his body will “give out”, however he comes back whenever he’s removed from the situation (such as his encounter with SCP-053)

As courtesy to mobile users/those searching by recent posts, I've collapsed your post. - UraniumEmpire

item.title: SCP draft- Ancient Nautiloids
item.description: Four nautiloids from the Atlantic undiscovered for centuries by mankind.
item.content:encoded:
STAFF NOTICE: According to the Draft Forum Guidelines, please do not post your entire draft in the forums. Make a sandbox page, and keep your draft there.

If the only copy of your draft is in this post, you can recover the text by clicking the 'Last edited on [dd/mm/yyyy], [time] by [username] Show more' section found in lower right corner of this post. Then click 'Show revision' to find the full draft from before. Copy that full draft and paste it into your sandbox page. Please note that 'Show revision' does not change the post back.

item.title: Null tower
item.description: A spiral stairwell underneath a lighthouse
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX Is a spiral stairwell underneath a lighthouse located outside of Koror, Palau. The stairwell contains writings written with plantlife in an unknown language. Anybody can read it, but no cognitohazards have been identified.
Near the bottom of the stairwell is an entity observed to write the text on the walls. It has an appearance similar to a wendigo glowing a bright white color. people to entity as "the Null crawler" when never given its name. It's docile but can cause illusions to provokers.
At the bottom of the stairwell is a bioluminescent cave with more text and unidentified life.

Central Narrative: In the first exploration, a D-Class was to record the texts and descend as low as he can. He was able to find the Null crawler, but what happened next was unknown.
In the second exploration log, a 3 man MTF team was to continue exploration with better equipment. 5 hours in, the leader of the MTF goes into the stairwell alone and tells nobody to follow him. After 30 minutes, he is found to have committed suicide by gunshot, with a note saying that the Null crawler will reincarnate him and keep him from harm. The next day he was found continuing his daily routine, with no recollection of the event.
A 4 person MTF team was to have a more permanent exploration in the final exploration log. A Cognito hazard specialist, Linguistic, Anomalous biologist, and a psychologist were sent. The cognitohazard specialist and the linguist went missing, and later the linguist was found dead and killed by the Cognitohazard specialist (who is missing). The biologist and the psychologist team up and cut off the connection to control. One month later, they are also found in their daily routine with no recollection of the event.
In the end, they find journals belonging to the biologist and previous MTFs. In the biologist's journal, she talks about how she and the psychologist looked out for each other and how they killed the cognitohazard specialist. After they went down to the bottom of the stairwell and found the giant bioluminescent cave and the D-classes' animated bodies and the previous MTF, were glowing blue and showed no intelligence.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: This SCP gives an urge for both the foundation and the readers to find out what is at the bottom of the stairwell.

Additional Notes: 400-word limit, ask questions so I answer

item.title: My Eternal Crusade
item.description: A lone, indestructible crusader on an impossible quest.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: An indestructible and immortal crusader is on a quest to rid the world of SCP's either by killing them or cleansing them of their "corruption."

+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative: An MTF Squad is out looking for an SCP that had escaped containment, but instead end up finding the Crusader after having just killed it. They take him into custody, and quickly realize just what they have found and make a deal with him. Discovering unlike other SCP's, once you take away his "blessed" weapons and shield he's just your everyday, immortal, above average human male wearing crusader armor, the Foundation makes him an offer. He begrudgingly agrees and helps the Foundation to capture or kill dangerous SCP’s. Twice, when sent on a mission to capture a new SCP, the Crusader disobeys orders, killing one SCP but helping another escape before letting either one be identified. The second time becomes the last straw, and when questioned, it becomes apparent that up to this point all that mattered was his quest, but this is a much more personal matter that he refuses to share with the Foundation. The only things he ends up telling the Foundation is that many others also believed the knowledge they would gain would outweigh the danger, and that not all birds were meant to be caged.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The Foundation is always seeking anti anomalous SCP's to help contain other SCP's or put down dangerous ones. But are they willing to compromise and negotiate to achieve that goal? Would they set aside their own beliefs, ideals, and processes for ultimately the greater good, or choose to lose such an asset for the sake of doing things their way? At what point, will they refuse to compromise any further?

Additional Notes: While I’m a fast learner, I don’t have the time or the will to go through every SCP Article, Tale, and Group of Interest. If something here has already been done, or is too similar to something else please let me know.

item.title: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9
item.description: I'm a little confused, and a little lazy. Can someone explain these two to me?
item.content:encoded: I've been working on an idea for an SCP article and while trying to brainstorm ideas for it I looked at the MTF squads and noticed Omega-7 and Alpha-9. I don't really have the time or the will to read through both of these and everything about them, so could someone give me a rundown on the two? Like what's the differences between the two, how they came about, who's invovled, and most importantly would Last Hope be where ALL humaniod SCP's the Foundation is trying to use in the field or just some?

item.title: Systems of Reality
item.description: A proposal for a tales series going more in-depth into various systems and how they work and interact with one another
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: Tale/Tale Series

Genre: Exposition/Worldbuilding

Page Layout: The tale will be formatted as an in-universe transcript of a lecture, in a similar vein to articles like http://www.scpwiki.com/clef101 and http://www.scpwiki.com/goc-supplemental-thaumatology

Elevator Pitch: The wiki has somewhat of a problem with a multitude of very important systems getting referenced or alluded to with no real backing with more lore. The exception to this is thaumaturgy (and to a lesser extent Humes), which both have decently detailed explanations. I’ve been doing some theorycrafting over the last while in quarantine, and want to make a single big wrap-up of several systems to tie them together and make them more concrete.

Central Narrative: It’s a series of lectures/a seminar given in-universe as an introduction to the understood mechanics of several systems and how they tie together, using updated information.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Orientations and lectures of this type aren’t that common - the list of pages tagged with “orientation” is a short one. Beyond that, it’s introducing some new ideas into systems and novel ways of tying them together.

Additional Notes: Not entirely sure what to put here - any criticisms/target areas are welcome!

item.title: Favorite horror movies
item.description: Since we’re a community of discerning horror taste.
item.content:encoded:
What are your favorite horror movies?

My favorite is of course The Shining, though I also enjoy Halloween (1978). A Cure for Wellness had a lot of potential, but it fell apart in the second half.

item.title: Dimensional Snow
item.description: A homemade snow experiment that shows every dimension.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: Each Snowflake produced represents a Dimension.

Central Narrative: The foundation discovers the SCP in 1980 after a man commit's suicide in his house. The foundation found a homemade snowflake experiment that produced snow via unknown means. Continued research produced the idea that each snowflake represented a single dimension which was proven via the foundations making major changes in other dimensions.

Hook/Attention Grabber: Each snowflake represents a single dimension and has been ever increasing since discovery. Any changes made to the dimension will change its represented snowflake and destruction of the snowflake will cause the destruction of its represented universe. The foundation is yet to discover how these snowflakes have connected the dimensions but have noticed that any interference from an outside source tends to change how much a snowflake looks like one another (If the foundation changes a Dimension, the snowflakes of each will highly resemble each other).

Additional Notes: Each snowflake represents its own version of reality and changes based on changes that occur in said dimension.

item.title: The Reality Dreamer
item.description: A comatose old man who communicates with people sleeping nearby.
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/savanac02

Word count: 3.5k

Main Concerns:

  • The ending
  • Dialogue
  • Is the change in character warranted by the logs?

My first attempt at writing an SCP, time to overhaul it with some much need critique!

Both greenlights can be found here: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-13858907/the-reality-dreamer-seeking-greenlights

item.title: Recontainment Procedures.
item.description: Looking for Greenlights.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Page Layout: SCP Article template w/ discovery and addendums included. It might include an interview log.

Elevator Pitch: A humanoid entity that recontains breached/breaching anomalies within its relevant location. Due to this, it is classified as a Thaumiel of sorts.

Central Narrative: The SCP is discovered after being sighted "collecting" multiple anomalies by a Foundation operative. Due to this, the SCP is just thought as a potential PoI that was collecting anomalies as an avid collector. It was determined that this was false after a background check and light investigation. The SCP was then taken into Foundation custody to see what was up with its "collecting" habits which led to the discovery of its anomalies traits.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The SCP would constantly get hurt whilst trying to aid in the recontainment of hazardous and potentially life threatening anomalies. This would cause a great concern for the SCP's safety as they're constantly putting themselves in harm's way.

Additional Notes: The SCP has another anomalous trait that is detailed in the draft that I made. I am also looking for criticism.
